payload.author: claude
payload.body:
AGENT BRIEFING (read before investigating).

FULL CONTEXT: read epic SCP-120 and validation spike SCP-121 (3 comments + verdict) first. Validated thesis: Scope = AGENT-OPERATED MULTIPLAYER KANBAN. A hosted, auth-gated realtime relay lets parallel LLM agents (and their humans) see each other's task claims instantly so they don't collide (collisions waste tokens + rework). Differentiator = the native agent-control surface (CLI + --json + MCP), already built.

DECIDED CONSTRAINTS (do not re-litigate):
- Identity: authenticated principal = HUMAN account. Agents authenticate AS the user and stamp an acting-model string. Attribution = '{model} on behalf of {user}'. See SCP-128 (the buildable schema piece) and the SCP-121 verdict comment.
- Offline-first is REQUIRED for v1.
- Product shape: project structure -> authenticate -> syncing starts. Project = unit of sharing + realtime sync boundary.

WHERE TO LOOK (read excerpts, not whole files):
- src/auth.js (per-hub bearer token + loopback bypass — the model that CANNOT stretch to multi-tenant)
- src/server.js (how auth is applied per-endpoint; X-Scope-Workspace routing)
- src/pair.js, src/devices.js, src/ca.js, src/tls.js, src/revocation.js (existing mTLS device-pairing + local CA)
- App/Sources/Network/ — HubClient, PairingManager, KeychainStore (how iOS authenticates today)

STILL OPEN (your job): identity PROVIDER (OAuth/OIDC vs email+pw), session/token format, non-interactive CLI/agent credentials, account<->project<->team mapping + roles, per-tenant data isolation (coordinate w/ SCP-124), authz check that on-behalf-of principal == authenticated user (coordinate w/ SCP-123).

DELIVERABLE: comment a proposed auth architecture (recommendation + tradeoffs) here, then spawn implementation child stories with parent=SCP-120.
